channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Business Leaders Tee Off to Support KIDZCAN’s Mission Across Zimbabwe @NetOneCellular
item.description: The inaugural NetOne-KIDZCAN Golf Tournament concluded recently, marking a significant event that transcended the greens to champion a noble cause: supporting children battling cancer. Engineer Raphael Mushanawani, Group Chief Executive... (via Zimetro News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimetro News

item.title: 72-Hour CAB 3 Ultimatum: Why Zimbabwe’s War Veterans Are Now Turning Against Zanu PF’s New Law... as Mnangagwa Summons Parliament @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Zimbabwe’s Political Quake: War Veterans Issue 72-Hour Ultimatum Over Controversial CAB3 Amidst Bribery Claims Harare – In an unprecedented display of defiance, a formidable group of Zimbabwe’s liberation war veterans has issued a stark 72-hour ultimatum to Parliament, demanding the immediate repeal of the contentious Constitution Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3). This audacious move, coming [...] The post 72-Hour CAB 3 Ultimatum: Why Zimbabwe’s War Veterans Are Now Turning Against Zanu PF’s New Law... as Mnangagwa Summons Parliament first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: The Prince of Harare: How Mnangagwa is rewriting the rules of political survival @nehandaradio
item.description: Gabriel Manyati examines Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 through the lens of political science, arguing that President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s latest reforms are less about governance than the enduring logic of political survival. The post The Prince of Harare: How Mnangagwa is rewriting the rules of political survival appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Luke-ing the Beast in the Eye: 27 June — when the cornered State went rogue and brutalized citizens for voting for change @nehandaradio
item.description: On the 18th anniversary of Zimbabwe’s disputed 27 June 2008 presidential run-off, Luke Tamborinyoka recounts his imprisonment, remembers victims of political violence and argues that CAB3 represents a new chapter in what he describes as state-sponsored attacks on democracy. The post Luke-ing the Beast in the Eye: 27 June — when the cornered State went rogue and brutalized citizens for voting for change appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Police Issue Fresh Warning As Popular Beer Habit Is Declared A Criminal Offence
item.description: Police Warn Thousands Could Be Breaking This Beer Law Without Realising It Many Zimbabweans have grown accustomed to asking bartenders to “put it on my tab” or promising to settle the bill later. However, police have now reminded both drinkers and liquor outlet operators that this common practice is a criminal offence under Zimbabwean law. [...] The post Police Issue Fresh Warning As Popular Beer Habit Is Declared A Criminal Offence appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: President Mnangagwa’s Nephew In Hot Water: Why A Gutu Tractor Rampage Has Police Scrambling For Answers
item.description: MASVINGO – In a disturbing turn of events that has ignited fresh debate over the rule of law in Zimbabwe, a close relative of President Emmerson Mnangagwa finds himself embroiled in a police investigation following a violent incident in Gutu. Local reports paint a vivid picture of chaos, where David Mnangagwa, identified as a nephew [...] The post President Mnangagwa’s Nephew In Hot Water: Why A Gutu Tractor Rampage Has Police Scrambling For Answers first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Retired Major General Everisto Dzihwema Dies After Treatment In India
item.description: Retired Major General Everisto Dzihwema Dies Aged 63 After Treatment In India Retired Zimbabwe National Army (ZNA) Major General Everisto Dzihwema has died at the age of 63 after receiving medical treatment in India. The former senior military officer died on Thursday, with the Zimbabwe National Army confirming his passing in a statement issued on [...] The post Retired Major General Everisto Dzihwema Dies After Treatment In India appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.description: Leading mobile network operator NetOne has reaffirmed its commitment to supporting healthcare and vulnerable communities after partnering with childhood cancer organisation KIDZCAN for an inaugural charity golf tournament aimed at raising awareness and support for children battling cancer. item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
